feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: How did a carpenter contract hepatitis C at work?
entry.summary: The work comp carrier denied Jack's claim, taking the position that he did not contract the Hepatitis C virus at work. That is when he came to see the experts at Just Work Comp Law.

entry.title: South Carolina's measles outbreak is over. But more are brewing around the country
entry.summary: The virus infected nearly 1,000 people in the state before the state declared it over. Meanwhile, cases are spreading across many parts of the U.S., with more than 20 outbreaks currently active.

entry.title: Scientists Discovered a Lost Population Near Paris, Solving 5,000-Year-Old Mystery
entry.summary: Ancient DNA from a megalithic tomb near Paris reveals a dramatic population collapse followed by the arrival of entirely new people.

entry.title: Eating fruits and veg can increase chance of lung cancer according to new study
entry.summary: A new study is raising questions about a possible link between healthy diets and lung cancer risk in younger non-smokers, with researchers pointing to pesticide exposure as a potential factor.

entry.title: CDC reports surge in antibiotic-resistant stomach bug
entry.summary: A common stomach bug is becoming harder to treat, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention says, adding to the growing list of infections that can withstand several lines of antibiotics. A CDC report released this month found “extensively drug-res…

entry.title: CDC warns of drug-resistant salmonella infections linked to backyard poultry
entry.summary: Federal health officials are investigating an outbreak of salmonella poisoning linked to backyard poultry that has sickened at least 34 people since February

entry.title: Children may be born with two complex cognitive functions already established, research reveals
entry.summary: A new study is the first to show that two of our most sophisticated cognitive functions, using and understanding language and being able to sense how other people feel, have distinct origins in the brain in young children—matching what we know about the adult…

entry.title: South Carolina’s measles outbreak is over after sickening nearly 1,000 people
entry.summary: South Carolina’s measles outbreak — the worst in the U.S. in more than 35 years — is over, state health officials declared Monday

entry.title: Scientists Discover “Molecular Switch” That Fuels Alzheimer’s Brain Inflammation
entry.summary: A newly identified trigger of brain inflammation could offer a fresh target for slowing Alzheimer’s progression.

entry.title: Calif. officials concerned as weekly average mpox cases 'more than double'
entry.summary: The California Department of Public Health is “strongly encouraging” at-risk individuals, including gay and bisexual men, to get vaccinated.

entry.title: Study: Infrasound likely a key factor in alleged hauntings
entry.summary: Low-frequency infrasound (below 20 Hz) can raise cortisol levels in saliva and increase irritability.

entry.title: GLP-1 drugs linked to osteoporosis, new research shows
entry.summary: GLP-1 drugs linked to osteoporosis, new research shows

entry.title: Fish Oil’s Brain Benefits Can Backfire, Study Suggests
entry.summary: A new study finds that some Omega-3s might not always be healthy for the brain under certain circumstances.

entry.title: CDC delay of infant hepatitis B shot likely to raise infections, studies show
entry.summary: Federal vaccine advisers to Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. voted in December to recommend delaying the first shot until at least two months of age for infants born to mothers who test negative for the virus.

entry.title: Looking for a healthy frozen meal? These 10 are dietitian-approved for days that you don't want to cook
entry.summary: Frozen dinners get a bad rap. But they're not all bad — you just have to know what to look for.

entry.title: Did you know stubborn belly fat can damage your liver? Doctor shares 5 daily habits that can take a toll on liver health | Health
entry.summary: Visceral fat is not only dangerous for your metabolic health but can also damage the liver. Dr Sood shares 5 habits that add strain on the organ. | Health

entry.title: Netizens Divided After Study Reveals Habit Practiced By 18 Million Americans Can Cause Permanent Brain Damage
entry.summary: “These differences may seem small at first, but they can add up in ways that affect learning, memory, and everyday functioning.”

entry.title: Common vitamin may help repair liver damage from a disease spreading worldwide
entry.summary: Vitamin E may repair liver damage caused by a silent disease, offering new hope to millions of people affected worldwide

entry.title: Bacterial defense system builds DNA in unexpected new way to stop viruses
entry.summary: Scientists at Stanford University have discovered that DRT3, a unique defense system found in bacteria, creates DNA to protect against viral infections. DRT3 is made up of two different enzymes called reverse transcriptases, Drt3a and Drt3b, and a piece of no…

entry.title: Are Mushrooms the Best Plant-Based Protein? A Dietitian Explains
entry.summary: Discover whether mushrooms are truly the best plant protein. Learn about protein quality, amino acids, digestibility, and how they compare to other sources.

entry.title: I'm a mobility coach: This 5-move 'barefoot' routine builds full-body balance and stability
entry.summary: Boost balance and connection with this simple routine

entry.title: Pomegranate Compound Could Help Protect Against Heart Disease
entry.summary: A compound produced by gut bacteria from pomegranate-derived molecules may play a critical role in protecting the cardiovascular system.

entry.title: A doctor shares 5 tips for better gut health in 30 days, including a trendy, low-cost supplement
entry.summary: A doctor who specializes in gut health said most people can feel better in weeks by making simple diet changes like adding a cheap fiber supplement.

entry.title: Sex scientist explains bizarre phenomenon of Ozempic penis and its impact on sex
entry.summary: In a recent episode of The Diary of a CEO podcast, sex expert Dr Rena Malik explained how drugs like Ozempic may affect libido and sexual function.

entry.title: This Common Vitamin Can Slash Your Risk of Developing Type 2 Diabetes
entry.summary: New research suggests that taking a vitamin D supplement could help keep people with prediabetes from developing type 2 diabetes.

entry.title: Want to Reduce the Risk of Cognitive Decline and Alzheimer's? Studies Say Do This Every Night
entry.summary: How much you sleep matters, but research shows poor sleep regularity is linked to greater risk of Alzheimer's.

entry.title: Certain Nap Patterns in Older Adults Could Be a Warning Sign, Study Finds
entry.summary: Naps can be amazing.

entry.title: Common Pregnancy Medications Linked to Higher Autism Risk in Massive U.S. Study
entry.summary: Prenatal use of certain common medications may raise autism risk, especially when multiple drugs are combined. Findings highlight the need for caution and further research.

entry.title: Scientists reveal the biological pathways linking childhood trauma to chronic gut pain
entry.summary: Experiencing stress during early childhood physically alters the nerves in the digestive system. Recent studies reveal how this biological remodeling causes chronic gut pain and movement issues, paving the way for highly targeted new therapies.

entry.title: What happened to Covid?
entry.summary: The thinking about what risk Covid poses — or doesn't pose — has shifted in recent years. What does it mean for you?

entry.title: Medieval art makes the body a site of theology and politics
entry.summary: Medieval artists depicted bodies as vehicles for politics and hierarchy. Repeated enough, these roles began to appear natural

entry.title: Safe sex concerns
entry.summary: Some Jamaicans say they are choosing abstinence as fears grow over rising condom prices, while others warn the cost could push risky behaviour. “Honestly, mi just ago abstain more than ever,” said Shanice Brown, a customer service representative...

entry.title: Patients say they want Alzheimer’s blood tests. Doctors aren’t sure they help.
entry.summary: The idea is straightforward: Take a blood test now, even without symptoms, and learn if you could some day develop Alzheimer’s disease

entry.title: Scientists search the microbiome for clues to the rise in colorectal cancers
entry.summary: Unlike many cancers, colorectal cancer has become more lethal for people at younger ages. Doctors are sleuthing out why.

entry.title: War and neglect fuel deadly measles epidemic in Sudan’s Darfur
entry.summary: Measles kills 70 in East Darfur’s Labado in a few weeks as healthcare collapse leaves families without medicines.

entry.title: Psychology says the single biggest predictor of happiness isn't income, relationships, or health
entry.summary: I want to tell you the most important finding in happiness research, and I want to warn you in advance that it’s going to sound obvious. It’s going to sound like something your grandmother would have told you. It’s going to sound like it doesn’t need a study …

entry.title: The mystery that led this family to get their stomachs removed : Short Wave
entry.summary: When Karyn Paringatai learned about a pattern among her family members, it changed the course of her life: A lot of people died young. As Karyn dug into her family history, she learned many Maori families, like her own, suffered from a rare form of stomach ca…

entry.title: The people who replay conversations for hours afterward aren't anxious. They're conducting a forensic review they were taught to perform as children, when missing a tonal shift in a parent's voice had real consequences
entry.summary: The forensic review of every conversation isn't a malfunction. It's a childhood-installed protocol running on adult relationships it was never designed for.

entry.title: Even Low Levels of Alcohol Could Damage Your Brain, Study Finds
entry.summary: We know that too much booze is bad for us, but a new study reveals that even a low level of alcohol intake could damage the brain.

entry.title: Your daily coffee habit could deliver real health benefits—if you make it this way
entry.summary: Pay attention to grinding, roasting, and brewing techniques and timings

entry.title: Signs of low magnesium levels: What doctors look for first
entry.summary: Health News: Most people have never had their magnesium levels checked. Their annual bloodwork probably didn't flag it. And yet, according to Dr. Divya Gopal, Addi.

entry.title: Getting breathless after a short walk? Why more young adults are facing this silent warning sign
entry.summary: There was a time when getting breathless after a short walk was seen in older adults or those with known illness. That picture is changing. Many young adults now report feeling out of breath after climbing a few stairs or walking a short distance. It often ge…

entry.title: Woman Handles Surgery And Recovery Solo, Husband Ignores Her Needs And Complains About Himself
entry.summary: A 28-year-old woman is fighting cervical cancer, but instead of facing it with support, she finds herself going through every appointment, biopsy, and surgery alone. As her diagnosis escalates to a tumor and she undergoes a LEEP procedure, her husband remai…

entry.title: Scientists identify the main cause of sunburns, and now they have to rewrite the textbooks
entry.summary: New research reveals that sunburn may be caused by RNA damage, not DNA, challenging long-held beliefs about UV exposure.

entry.title: Scientists Say “Grandma Brain” Is Real — Here’s What That Means
entry.summary: A grandparent's love is truly like nothing else. Scientists explain what "grandma brain" is and what exactly causes that unique emotion.

entry.title: Judy Foreman: One Scan, One Shock, One Mission for Santa Barbara Native Shira Kupperman Boehler
entry.summary: On a recent Sunday morning, Drs. Nancy and Jeffrey Kupperman opened their Montecito home to about 50 close friends and family for a brunch unlike any

entry.title: Finland replaced artificial playground surfaces with natural elements like mud and soil — and the results surprised even researchers
entry.summary: The goal was to increase children's exposure to environmental microbes through everyday play.

entry.title: US health officials nix publication of a study on Covid vaccine effectiveness
entry.summary: U.S. health officials stopped the publication of a study on whether the Covid-19 vaccine was keeping adults from becoming sick enough to have to go to the hospital

entry.title: NJ warns of possible measles exposures at Newark Airport
entry.summary: A Hudson County resident was diagnosed with measles after returning from an international trip, the state health department said.

entry.title: Even light drinking combined with aging is linked to reduced brain blood flow and thinner tissue
entry.summary: A new study suggests that even low-level drinking over a lifetime may negatively affect brain health. Researchers found that cumulative light drinking, combined with aging, is linked to reduced blood flow and thinner tissue in the brain.
